title: 10 best herbal teas for heartburn

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tea have antioxidant and anti-irritant effects, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Licoricidin: This compound has been shown to have gastroprotective properties, which can help reduce stomach acid production and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with heartburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the digestive system. The roots of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can be a major contributor to heartburn. Additionally, licorice tea has been shown to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, which can help neutralize acid and provide relief from heartburn symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate heartburn and digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Flavonoids: Contributes to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra, which helps in soothing the esophageal lining and reducing acid production in the stomach.
Licoricidin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, thus providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties to help soothe heartburn and digestive issues.
Leaves: They contain glycosides that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and soothing effects to help alleviate heartburn and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when roots are mature.
Crush the dried roots into smaller pieces to release their active compounds and increase surface area.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan and steep 1/2 teaspoon of crushed roots for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots.
Allow the tea to cool and drink it within 30 minutes to 1 hour for maximum benefits.

remedy_properties: Antacid Properties: Peppermint tea has antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from heartburn symptoms such as burning chest pain and discomfort.

Relaxation of Smooth Muscle: Peppermint's menthol content helps relax the smooth muscle in the esophagus, allowing food to pass through the digestive system more easily and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to heartburn.
Inflammation Reduction: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with heartburn because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation in the esophagus. The menthol in peppermint tea relaxes the muscles in the stomach, allowing food to pass through more easily and preventing acid reflux. Additionally, peppermint tea has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to regulate gut function and alleviate symptoms of heartburn, providing quick relief for those who suffer from this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes.
Menthol: As a terpene, menthol helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Rosmarinic acid (Phenolic acid): This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus from acid damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are used to make teas for heartburn because they contain menthol and other compounds that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract.
Buds: Mentha x piperita buds are used to make teas for heartburn because they contain oils that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are also used to make teas for heartburn because they have a calming effect on the stomach, reducing acid production and relieving discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of fresh or dried mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in a clean container.
Steep the mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea if desired and stir to combine.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy warm or at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. remedy_recipe: Choose high-quality zingiber officinale rhizomes with minimal moisture content and store them in a cool dry place.
Cut 1 to 2 inches of fresh zingiber officinale rhizome into thin slices with a sharp knife.
Combine 1 teaspoon of sliced zingiber officinale with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes or until the desired flavor and aroma are achieved.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi leaves and stems in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the carum carvi leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 teaspoons of dried carum carvi leaves and stems into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground carum carvi in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the carum carvi tea and enjoy it as a hot beverage immediately.
